title: Web build cannot write the standard fidelity screenshot (sync GPU map-wait fails under Emscripten), and its render-frame ordinal does not track the sim clock -- neither flag can pin a web capture
suspect: src/platform/platform_sdl.c:1053 (platformSaveScreenshot -> gfx_backend_read_framebuffer_rgb; bails with '[gfx] screenshot readback failed')
src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c:3684 (WGPU_COMPAT_WAIT on wgpuBufferMapAsync -- the map callback only fires during ProcessEvents, which cannot run inside this synchronous call on web)
repro: Serve dist/web, boot with ?arg=--level&arg=dam&arg=--deterministic&arg=--screenshot-game-timer&arg=300. The engine reaches the timer and attempts the capture, but the console shows '[gfx] screenshot readback failed' and no BMP is written to MEMFS (FS.readdir('/') shows no screenshot_*.bmp). The same flags on the native binary write screenshot_<label>.bmp normally. Harness: .superpowers/sdd/task-6-harness/webprobe6.mjs --mode timer.

evidence.path: src/platform/platform_sdl.c:1053 -- Task 6, web dist built at 88c7f31, headless Chrome, canvas pinned to a 640x480 backing store. Console tail ends with '[gfx] screenshot readback failed'; MEMFS listing after the timer is reached is {'/':['.','..','tmp','home','dev','proc','rom','save'],'/tmp':['.','..']} -- no screenshot written. The WebGPU PPM dump path (GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_FRAME / GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE) DOES work on web ('[webgpu] wrote frame dump /tmp/webgpu_frame_600.ppm (640x480)'), because it maps a buffer whose copy was already submitted in the same end_frame rather than issuing its own mid-frame encoder + blocking wait. So web fidelity capture must go through the dump path, not the screenshot path.

src/game/lvl.c:2203 -- the web render-frame ordinal is NOT proportional to the sim clock. [CORRECTED, Final-review fix round 2026-07-20: this entry originally read '...and the ratio is not even stable across runs of the same build ... Non-monotonic across runs', which is self-contradictory and clashes with the engine's own determinism -- within a single run g_ClockTimer >= 0 makes the timer monotonic non-decreasing in frame ordinal, so 234/1039/471 at frames 400/600/800 cannot all come from one run, and Task 6 separately reproduced the frame-600 dump byte-identically (same md5) across two independent runs of that SAME configuration (task-6-report.md Sec 3.3) -- i.e. a FIXED configuration IS deterministic. The corrected, true statement follows.] The frame->timer map is NOT REPRODUCIBLE ACROSS CONFIGURATIONS: each of the three numbers below came from a SEPARATE run of the build using a different GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE=N value (that env var names a single one-shot dump target per process, so three dump points require three process launches), and changing which frame is dumped changes how much work the engine has done by that point -- so the load profile, and with it the frame:ticks ratio, differs configuration to configuration, not run to run of the *same* configuration. Task 6 placed three web GPU dumps on the native sky curve (see FID-0134) by least-squares: web render frame 400 (its own run) -> native game timer 234; frame 600 (a separate run, itself reproduced byte-identically across two further runs of that same N=600 configuration) -> timer 1039; frame 800 (a third run) -> timer 471. Cause: g_GlobalTimer/g_SkyCloudOffset accumulate g_ClockTimer (0 while controls are locked, up to 4 on a hitch) per rendered frame, so the frames:ticks ratio is load-profile-dependent. The same effect is visible natively: with GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_FRAME, a native run that reaches game timer 300 has a backend frame_no between 50 and 200 (dumps fire for N=5 and N=50, not for N=200). CONSEQUENCE (unaffected by this correction): the Task-4 assumption of a fixed '+200' native/web frame-ordinal offset is invalid; frame ordinals cannot pin a web capture at all -- independently corroborated by this native frame_no 50-200 spread and by FID-0134's Blocker 1/2 findings.

.superpowers/sdd/task-6-harness/analyze.py -- the workaround actually used (locus matching against a timer-pinned native curve) is implemented here; full write-up in .superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md (sections 'Method' and 'Residual (b): sky hue').

history.evidence: Task 6: web --screenshot-game-timer produces '[gfx] screenshot readback failed' and no MEMFS file, while the GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_* PPM path succeeds in the same run; three web dumps (three separate run configurations, one GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE=N each) fit native game timers 234/1039/471 at render frames 400/600/800 -- not reproducible across configurations, though a fixed configuration (N=600, re-run twice) reproduced byte-identically. See the Final-review fix round history entry below for the corrected wording.
history.note: Filed by Task 6. Two coupled instrumentation gaps that together mean a web frame cannot be pinned the way a native frame can. (1) The screenshot path needs a synchronous GPU buffer map; on web the map callback only fires during ProcessEvents, which cannot run inside that blocking call, so every web screenshot fails closed. (2) Even with a working screenshot, --screenshot-frame is a render-frame counter whose relation to the sim clock is load-dependent, so it cannot align a web capture to a native one. Task 6 worked around both by (a) capturing the web via GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE and (b) locating the web frame on a native curve that IS timer-pinned, using the sky as the clock and the frame-invariant terrain as a registration control. Durable fixes, both deferred and not implemented here: make the web screenshot path async (or route it through the same deferred write the dump path uses), and emit g_GlobalTimer into the dump filename or a sidecar so a web dump is self-identifying. Until then, treat any web-vs-native pixel comparison that pins on a frame ordinal as unregistered.

history.evidence: Final-review fix round (fix/tier1-residuals): re-derived which runs produced 234/1039/471 from .superpowers/sdd/task-6-report.md Sec 1.1/3.3 (three separate GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE=N runs; the N=600 run separately reproduced byte-identically across two further runs of that same config).
history.note: Final-review fix round, 2026-07-20. Corrected a self-contradictory phrase in evidence[1] above: 'the ratio is not even stable across runs of the same build ... Non-monotonic across runs' cannot be true of a single run (g_ClockTimer >= 0 makes the timer monotonic non-decreasing in frame ordinal within one run) and clashes with the report's own determinism finding (frame 600 reproduced byte-identically, same md5, across two runs of that configuration). The three numbers 234/1039/471 in fact came from THREE SEPARATE RUNS, each with a different GE007_WEBGPU_DUMP_SURFACE=N (400/600/800 respectively) -- changing which frame is dumped changes the engine's load profile up to that point, hence a different frame:ticks ratio per configuration. Reworded to: the frame->timer map is NOT REPRODUCIBLE ACROSS CONFIGURATIONS, while a FIXED configuration IS deterministic. The finding's conclusion is UNCHANGED and was never resting on the retracted phrase alone -- it is independently supported by Blocker 1 (the screenshot path fails closed on web regardless) and by the native frame_no 50-200 corroboration for game timer 300, both cited in evidence[1].
